title: Standalone checksum-generation script for release assets
action: Create scripts/generate-checksums.sh: given a directory path of packaged release assets, compute SHA-256 for each release archive and write a single checksums.txt in sha256sum-compatible format (one `<sha256>  <filename>` line per asset) to STDOUT ONLY (the caller decides where to redirect it -- never write the output file directly inside the scanned directory, since a self-included output file corrupts the checksum set). Only match files ending in `.tar.gz` or `.zip` -- skip subdirectories entirely (a real release dist directory contains an extracted `$name/` folder alongside the archives, per release.yml:79-91) and skip any non-matching file. Must run correctly on both Linux and macOS bash using whichever hashing tool is present (sha256sum on Linux, shasum -a 256 on macOS where sha256sum is absent) -- detect which is available and use it. This is scaffolding for self-update-merge-config's checksum-verification feature (per D8, D10 in docs/history/self-update-merge-config/CONTEXT.md) -- do not modify .github/workflows/release.yml in this cell, only create the standalone script.

truths: Running the script against a directory containing sample .tar.gz/.zip files plus an unrelated subdirectory produces output covering only the archive files -- the subdirectory is skipped, not treated as a file
Running the script's output through sha256sum -c / shasum -a 256 -c verifies successfully for each archive file
The script works in an environment with only shasum present (no sha256sum) and in one with only sha256sum present

artifacts.substantive: executable script, no TODO stubs, detects and uses whichever of sha256sum/shasum is available, writes only to stdout

prohibitions: No changes to .github/workflows/release.yml in this cell
Never writes its output file inside the directory it is scanning

verify: bash -c 'set -e; d=$(mktemp -d); out=$(mktemp); echo hello > "$d/a.tar.gz"; echo world > "$d/b.zip"; mkdir "$d/herdr-go-x86_64"; echo notanasset > "$d/herdr-go-x86_64/herdr-go"; bash scripts/generate-checksums.sh "$d" > "$out"; cd "$d"; sha256sum -c "$out" || shasum -a 256 -c "$out"; test $(wc -l < "$out") -eq 2'

trace.outcome: Created scripts/generate-checksums.sh: detects sha256sum or shasum -a 256, hashes only top-level *.tar.gz/*.zip files (skips subdirectories), prints sha256sum-compatible lines to stdout only
